authMethods.description: Run `opencode auth login` in the terminal
authMethods.name: Login with opencode

availableModes.name: build
availableModes.description: The default agent. Executes tools based on configured permissions.

availableModes.name: plan
availableModes.description: Plan mode. Disallows all edit tools.

availableCommands.name: frontend-design
availableCommands.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

availableCommands.name: browse
availableCommands.description:
Browse and interact with web pages using Chrome DevTools MCP. Use this skill when:
(1) Navigating to URLs to inspect web pages
(2) Taking screenshots of web pages
(3) Clicking elements, filling forms, or interacting with UI
(4) Inspecting DOM, console logs, or network requests
(5) Debugging frontend issues or testing web applications
(6) Verifying UI rendering or behavior
Requires Chrome running with --remote-debugging-port=9222

availableCommands.name: skill-installer
availableCommands.description: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos).

availableCommands.name: ask-questions-if-underspecified
availableCommands.description: Clarify requirements before implementing. Use when serious doubts araise.

availableCommands.name: skill-creator
availableCommands.description: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.

availableCommands.name: go-code-quality
availableCommands.description: Comprehensive Go code quality assistance including documentation with go doc, code formatting, and modern Go idioms for Go 1.25+. Use when working with Go codebases for retrieving symbol documentation, formatting code consistently, learning modern Go best practices, and writing well-documented Go code.

Title: Canada - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canada
Text: Canada

Canada is a country in North America. Its ten provinces and three territories extend from the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean and northward into the Arctic Ocean, making it the second-largest country by total area, with the longest coastline of any country. Its border with the United States is the longest international land border. The country is characterized by a wide range of both meteorologic and geological regions. With a population of over 41 million, it has widely varying population densities, with the majority residing in its urban areas and large areas being sparsely populated. Its capital is Ottawa and its three largest metropolitan areas are Toronto, Montreal, and Vancouver.

Indigenous peoples have continuously inhabited what is now Canada for thousands of years. Beginning in the 16th century, British and French expeditions explored and later settled along the Atlantic coast. As a conseq

Title: Demographics of Canada
Author: Van00220
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographics_of_Canada
Text: Demographics of Canada

Statistics Canada conducts a country-wide census that collects demographic data every five years on the first and sixth year of each decade. The 2021 Canadian census enumerated a total population of 36,991,981, an increase of around 5.2 percent over the 2016 figure. It is estimated that Canada's population surpassed 40 million in 2023 and 41 million in 2024. The main driver of population growth is immigration, with 6.2% of the country's population being made up of temporary residents as of 2023, or about 2.5 million people. In 2025, Canada's population officially stood at 41.5 million, a decrease of approximately −76,068 people, With the lowest recorded total fertility rate in the country's history in 2024, at around 1.25, also one of the world's lowest.

Canada has one of the highest per-capita immigration rates in the world, driven mainly by economi

### Welcome to Canada: Understanding the Land and Its People

As you embark on this new chapter of your life in Canada, it’s important to understand the history and geography that shape this vast and diverse country. Canada is more than just a place to live and work; it’s a nation with a rich history, a vibrant culture, and a dynamic environment that will influence your personal and professional journey.

### A Brief History of Canada

Canada’s history is a tapestry woven with stories of Indigenous peoples, Eur

Title: Understanding Canada: History, Culture, and Business Insights
URL: https://aperian.com/blog/understanding-canada-looking-beyond-the-headlines/
Text: Understanding Canada: History, Culture, and Business Insights | Aperian

# Understanding Canada: Looking Beyond the Headlines

Canada is part of the same North American continent as the United States and Mexico, and its economy has become closely integrated with both. However, at a time when longstanding commercial relationships are under scrutiny—including ties with Canadian customers, suppliers, and consumers—it is also important to understand how Canada is distinctive. Key social factors such as demographics, history, and immigration patterns help to explain how Canadians see themselves and the range of issues they face today.

The classic intercultural mistake of “assumed similarity,” or projecting one’s own expectations onto the residents of another country, has plagued U.S. interactions with Canada for centuries, although mu

Title: 9.5 Canada – Introduction to World Regional Geography
URL: https://slcc.pressbooks.pub/worldgeography/chapter/9-5/
Text: 9.5 Canada – Introduction to World Regional Geography "

North America

# Canadian Provinces and Territories

Canada’s democratic state shares a similar developmental history and economic status with the United States. As of 2011, Canada had a population of just over thirty-four million (2011), which is less than California’s population. Canada is larger than the United States, making it the second-largest country in the world. However, despite this vast territory for a relatively small population, more than 90 percent of Canadians live within 150 miles of the US border. Northern Canada is not considered part of Canada’s ecumene, or habitable zone, for permanent human settlement. Only a narrow band of territory in southern and eastern Canada has the climate and physical geography suitable for agricultural production and widespread settlement.

Moreover, Cana

Text: Canada — World Factbook

# Canada

North America Sovereign GEC: CA ISO: CA

Compare Timeline JSON Print

## Introduction

### Background

A land of vast distances and rich natural resources, Canada became a self-governing dominion in 1867, while retaining ties to the British crown. Canada gained legislative independence from Britain in 1931 and formalized its constitutional independence from the UK when it passed the Canada Act in 1982. Economically and technologically, the nation has developed in parallel with the US, its neighbor to the south across the world's longest international border. Canada faces the political challenges of meeting public demands for quality improvements in health care, education, social services, and economic competitiveness, as well as responding to the particular concerns of predominantly francopho

Its border with the United States is the longest international land border. The country is characterized by a wide range of both meteorologic and geological regions. With a population of over 41 million, it has widely varying population densities, with the majority residing in its urban areas and large areas being sparsely populated. Its capital is Ottawa and its three largest metropolitan areas are Toronto, Montreal, and Vancouver.

content.text: The user wants me to talk about Canada in great detail, starting with "Canada is
". Let me compile a comprehensive response based on my knowledge and
the search results.
Canada is a vast and diverse country

in North America, renowned for its stunning natural landscapes, multicultural society, and high quality of life. Here's a comprehensive overview:

## Geography and Size

Canada is the **second-largest country
by total area** in the world, covering approximately 9.97 million square kilometers.

It spans:

- **4,600 km from north to south**
- **5,500 km from east

to west**
- **The longest coastline of any country** in the

world
- **The longest international land border** with the

United States (approximately 8,891 km)

The country stretches

from the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean and northward into the Arctic Ocean, encompassing six time zones.

## Provinces and

Territories

Canada comprises **10 provinces** and **3 territories**:

- **
Provinces**: Alberta, British Columbia, Manitoba, New Brunswick, Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia,

Ontario, Prince Edward Island, Quebec, Saskatchewan
- **Territories**: Northwest Territories, Nunavut,

Yukon

## Population and Demographics

- Population: Approximately **41.5 million** (as of 2025)
- Over **90% of Canadians**

live within 150 miles of the U.S. border
- Major metropolitan areas

: Toronto, Montreal, Vancouver, Calgary, Edmonton, Ottawa
- One of the highest per-cap
ita immigration rates globally
- Fertility rate: Approximately 1.25 (one of the world's

lowest)

## Capital and Major Cities

- **Capital**: Ottawa
- **Largest cities**: Toronto, Montreal, Vancouver

## Official Languages

- **
English** and **French**

(bilingual official languages)
- French is predominant in Quebec
- Indigenous languages are also recognized

## Government

- Federal parliamentary state and constitutional monarchy
- Queen Charles III is the head of state
- Prime Minister serves as head of government
- Achieved self-governing dominion status in **1867**
- Gained

full legislative independence in **1931**
- Constitutional independence formalized with
the **Canada Act 1982**

## Economy

- One of the world's wealthiest

nations
- Highly integrated with the U.S. economy
- Key industries: Natural resources (oil
, minerals, timber), manufacturing, technology,

financial services, aerospace, agriculture
- Currency: **Canadian dollar (

CAD)**
- Significant trade partner with the United States (USMCA)

## Natural Geography

### Mountain Ranges
- Rocky Mountains
- Coast Mountains
- Laurentian Mountains

### Major Rivers
- St

. Lawrence River
- Mackenzie River

### Other Features
- Glaciers (especially in the Arctic and Alpine regions)
- Forests covering nearly

50% of the land
- Thousands of lakes (including the Great Lakes)

## Climate

Canada exhibits extreme climatic

diversity:
- **Arctic** in the far north (tundra)
-
**Subarctic** in the central

regions
- **Continental** in the south

- **Pacific coastal** in British Columbia
- Significant seasonal variation, with cold winters

and warm summers in most regions

## History

### Indigenous Peoples
- Indigenous peoples have

inhabited Canada for **thousands of years**
- First Nations, Inuit,

and Métis peoples

### European Exploration and Settlement
- **16th century**: British and French expeditions began exploring the Atlantic

coast
- **1608**: French settlement

of Quebec City
- **1763**: Treaty of Paris ended Seven Years' War, French

Canada became British
- **1774**: Quebec Act allowed French culture and

legal rights

###

Confederation and Development
- **1867**: Confederation of Canada (British North America Act)
- **1871-1949**:

Progressive addition of provinces
- **World Wars**: Significant contributions and growth
- **1960s

-1980s**: Modernization and constitutional developments

## Culture and Society

- **Multiculturalism**

is official policy
- Strong emphasis on **social programs** (universal healthcare, education)
- Values: Equality,

diversity, bilingualism, reconciliation with Indigenous peoples
- Famous for hockey, maple syrup,
and the Canadian Rockies

- Civil law in Quebec, common law elsewhere

##

Indigenous Peoples

- First Nations (distinct nations and territories)
- Inuit (Arctic regions)
- Métis (mixed Indigenous

-European heritage)
- Ongoing efforts

toward **reconciliation** and ** Indigenous rights**

## Education and Quality of Life

- World

-class education system
- High literacy rates
- Universal healthcare system
- High standards of

living
- Known for safety and

political stability

## International Relations

- Active member

of the Commonwealth of Nations
- Founding member of NATO and G7
- Significant role

in United Nations peacekeeping
- Strong U.S. bilateral relations

- Leadership in environmental protection and climate action

---

Canada is
characterized by its remarkable diversity—both geographical and
human—a strong commitment to multiculturalism, and a high standard of living. It balances vast wilderness with sophisticated urban centers, and maintains
a unique identity shaped by its Indigenous heritage, French and British colonial history, and waves of immigration from around the
world.
